content.problemStatement:
A complete bipartite graph or biclique is a special kind of bipartite graph. If we denote the two partitions of vertices of this graph by AAA and BBB, then such a biclique is denoted by K‚ą£A‚ą£,‚ą£B‚ą£K_{|A|, |B|}K‚ą£A‚ą£,‚ą£B‚ą£‚Äč and satisfies the following conditions:


For each pair of vertices a,ba, ba,b (a‚ąąAa \in Aa‚ąąA, b‚ąąBb \in Bb‚ąąB), there is an edge between vertices aaa and bbb.
For each pair of vertices u,v‚ąąAu, v \in Au,v‚ąąA, there is no edge between these vertices.
For each pair of vertices u,v‚ąąBu, v \in Bu,v‚ąąB, there is no edge between these vertices either.
You are given a simple graph with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM edges. Note that this graph is not necessarily bipartite. You are also given an integer kkk. Determine whether the graph contains the biclique K2,kK_{2, k}K2,k‚Äč as a subgraph at least once, i.e. whether it is possible to obtain K2,kK_{2, k}K2,k‚Äč by removing some (possibly zero) vertices, all edges adjacent to the removed vertices and some (possibly zero) more edges.
constraints: the graph does not contain any self-loops or multiple edges
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and kkk.
Each of the following MMM lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that there is an edge between vertices uuu and vvv.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if the graph contains K2,kK_{2, k}K2,k‚Äč or `"NO"` if it does not (without quotes).
